summary: Stellar Dark Star jam, bright Here Comes Sunshine, tight first set
blurb: The Utica show delivers a vibrant first set with highlights like Bertha and Jack Straw, while the second set erupts into an expansive Dark Star > Eyes of the World sequence. Jerry’s lead work shines on Here Comes Sunshine and the jam flow feels organic throughout.
review: On March 21, 1973 at Utica Memorial Auditorium the Dead were in top form. The first set rolls out a crisp mix of classics – Bertha opens with gusto, Me And My Uncle and Wave That Flag keep things lively, and Here Comes Sunshine finds Jerry’s guitar soaring over Keith’s tasteful keyboards. Jack Straw and China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider add that mid‑set sparkle before the band dives into a second‑set marathon. Dark Star launches into an exploratory journey that seamlessly folds into Eyes Of The World, showcasing a deep improvisational well, while the rest of the set—Box of Rain, Me And Bobby McGee and a heartfelt Wharf Rat—keeps the momentum high. Despite minor tape hum and occasional vocal clipping, the soundboard capture is clear enough to let the band’s chemistry shine, making this a must‑listen for anyone chasing early‑’73 magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising the Dark Star > Eyes of the World jam, Here Comes Sunshine and overall band energy; one recording is mixed but still notes strong moments. The consensus is strongly favorable, warranting a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Good soundboard source with clear balance, captures the full show and highlights despite minor hum; widely praised as the most complete and sonically reliable version.

key_highlights: Dark Star > Eyes Of The World jam showcases expansive improvisation.
Here Comes Sunshine features standout lead work from Jerry and bright keyboards.
Tight first‑set flow with Bertha, Jack Straw and China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider.
Keith’s slipknot‑riff doodle adds a unique flavor to the mid‑set jams.

band_performance.Jerry: Beautiful playing throughout, especially on Here Comes Sunshine and the Dark Star jam; occasional mix burying but his lead shines.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with strong moments on Box of Rain; vocals noted as strong for this era.
band_performance.Bob: Stunning lead guitar on Here Comes Sunshine, adds backing vocal texture; at times a bit forward in the mix.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s keyboards drive the jam energy, highlighted by the slipknot‑riff doodle and soaring lines on Jack Straw.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is essential to the improvisational flow, praised for keeping the second‑set momentum tight.
